summary: RETEST after iteration-1 fixes. ALL three reported fixes are confirmed working on the Expo web preview: (1) Tab testIDs tab-road / tab-stats / tab-settings now render as data-testid in the DOM via the custom tabBarButton (Pressable) wrapper — clicking each navigates to home-screen / stats-screen / settings-screen correctly. (2) QuotaExceededError is GONE from console logs — AsyncStorage no longer tries to cache the 6 MB asset pack; assets still load (runner image visible on onboarding slide 1, habit card icons render). (3) shadow* and Image.style.resizeMode deprecation warnings are GONE. One residual deprecation warning remains: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents' (4 occurrences). RoadTile.tsx was correctly fixed, so this warning now originates from React Navigation / Animated.View internals (library-level), not app code — non-blocking. Full happy-path executed end-to-end: onboarding-skip → add-habit-fab → add-habit-name='Read Daily' → add-habit-save → habit card renders → done-today toggles header-stats 0/1 → 1/1 → 0/1 → tab-stats (stats-screen visible) → tab-settings (settings-screen visible) → setting-reset → setting-reset-confirm (data wiped, back to empty 'No road yet'). Backend not retested (was 100% in iteration 1, no backend changes).

issues: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents' still appears 4× in console. Source is no longer our code (RoadTile.tsx was fixed) — it originates from React Navigation's internal Animated.View on the tab bar. Non-blocking; can only be fully silenced by upgrading react-navigation.

action_items: OPTIONAL/LOW: Upgrade @react-navigation/bottom-tabs to a version that uses style.pointerEvents internally to silence the last deprecation warning. Not required for functionality.

OPTIONAL: Long-term, consider persisting AI PNG assets to expo-file-system (~6 MB pack is now only in-memory and re-fetched from backend MongoDB on cold start — acceptable for current online-first warm-up, but offline cold-launch will fall back to MaterialCommunityIcons placeholders).
critical_code_review_comments: (tabs)/_layout.tsx — custom Pressable tabBarButton correctly forwards testID to a real DOM node on web. Good fix.
src/lib/assets.ts — AsyncStorage import removed; pack is fetched fresh from /api/assets and held in memory only. Backend already caches in MongoDB, so cold-start cost is one fetch.
RoadTile.tsx already uses style.pointerEvents; (tabs)/index.tsx FAB uses boxShadow; onboarding.tsx Image uses prop resizeMode='contain'. All three fixes confirmed in source.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — all spec testIDs present, happy-path passes, no QuotaExceededError, no blocking warnings

seed_data_creation: None. Habit 'Read Daily' created and removed via Reset all data within the test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 2 PASSED. Tab testIDs are now exposed via custom Pressable tabBarButton in (tabs)/_layout.tsx — selector [data-testid='tab-road|tab-stats|tab-settings'] works. AsyncStorage caching of asset pack has been removed (only in-memory + backend MongoDB cache). The residual 'props.pointerEvents' warning is from react-navigation internals (not our code) — safe to ignore. To reset state in tests, run localStorage.clear() then reload — onboarding will reappear.
rca_of_issue: All three iteration-1 issues resolved at the correct layers: (a) testID forwarding done by replacing the broken tabBarTestID flow with a controlled Pressable tabBarButton; (b) storage quota eliminated by deleting the AsyncStorage write path entirely and relying on the server-side MongoDB cache; (c) deprecation warnings fixed at each component (FAB→boxShadow, Image→prop resizeMode, RoadTile→style.pointerEvents).
